message:
fix: ensure FK validation queries see current transaction state after prepared stmt reuse

Add dedicated fk_validation_query() execution path that forces a
MemDatabase refresh and discards the cached VDBE engine before running
FK parent/child existence checks. This guarantees fresh storage cursors
with no retained B-tree seek caches from prior operations.

Root cause: when a prepared statement was reused across multiple
iterations in a write transaction (e.g., BFS graph traversal), then
DML operations modified data, the subsequent FK validation SELECT could
read stale cursor/page-cache state and report false "FOREIGN KEY
constraint failed" errors for rows that exist in the current transaction.

Applied to check_fk_parent_exists, check_fk_on_delete, and
check_fk_on_update. Includes three regression tests reproducing the
exact scenario from the bug report.

Closes #59

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
